title: Old timers Why do you keep coming back to Eva
display_title: Old timers Why do you keep coming back to Eva
wikitext:
[]: Just what the heck is it about eva anyway?             Quote Post by DatDude » Sun Nov 21, 2004 7:39 pm We've all watched it, we all thought about it, and we've all talked about it to death on this page alone. Just what the heck keeps us all into eva ? For me I think its that the way the story is told you really have to think about it inorder to get it fully, and the fact that I can identify with a lot of the characters. THat or im stark raving mad and this is just what crazy people do . . .

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sun Nov 21, 2004 7:44 pm 1. I woud have to agree with you on the wa the story is told. 2. The amount of information to be researched also keeps us going too. 3. And to a leser extent ( 1%) the fan service. i think this is a good question to ask your self "Why do u keep watching eva and why do u keep coming back to the forums" My anwser is point 2

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Nov 21, 2004 8:29 pm To me, it boils down to the characters. That's part of the reason why I prefer the series ending to the End of Eva. At the end of it all, Evangelion is not about Angels or Evas or any of that. Its about the characters. And every one of them is well defined, well portrayed, and in some way relatable to ones self.

[]:             Quote Post by Ayanami » Sun Nov 21, 2004 8:36 pm I've been thinking about that for awhile. I mean, there are many other series other than Eva that I'm just as crazy for, but just... something about Eva makes me want to know more and ask "why?" a lot more than those other series do. Maybe it's the mysterious religious symbolism, although I'm not much of a religious person, myself. Maybe the complexity of the characters. Maybe the intriguing storyline. Y'know, I honestly don't know. But that's really how you know something is really, truly good. You keep going back to it, and are constantly finding things you never noticed, even after like, say, 10 years. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Sun Nov 21, 2004 9:06 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: To me, it boils down to the characters. That's part of the reason why I prefer the series ending to the End of Eva. At the end of it all, Evangelion is not about Angels or Evas or any of that. Its about the characters. And every one of them is well defined, well portrayed, and in some way relatable to ones self.

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI » Sun Nov 21, 2004 9:54 pm For me, it's because you know, deep inside, there's always more to it. There's always some meaning or thing or history behind the things we see, and we'll try to work it out. We've got 26 episodes, two movies and some other miscellaneous things to work from. I guess it's that we want to know more about it, and there seems to be a way to do so. That's why Anno said he won't tell you everything about Eva, because that would kill it.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Nov 22, 2004 6:13 pm In my opinion (and this is not an attack on the starter of this thread), this has all the meaning of a Christian thread asking, "So why do YOU love Jesus?"

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Nov 22, 2004 9:59 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: In my opinion (and this is not an attack on the starter of this thread), this has all the meaning of a Christian thread asking, "So why do YOU love Jesus?"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 23, 2004 12:58 am The characters are okay. But the Evas and the Angels are the best part, despite whatever Aaron might try to drive into your skull. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Nov 24, 2004 4:14 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: To me, it boils down to the characters. That's part of the reason why I prefer the series ending to the End of Eva. At the end of it all, Evangelion is not about Angels or Evas or any of that. Its about the characters. And every one of them is well defined, well portrayed, and in some way relatable to ones self.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Sun Nov 28, 2004 9:23 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: In my opinion (and this is not an attack on the starter of this thread), this has all the meaning of a Christian thread asking, "So why do YOU love Jesus?"

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI » Mon Nov 29, 2004 12:00 am That's sort of saying what I just said... -___-;

[]:             Quote Post by Otaprince » Mon Nov 29, 2004 12:53 am The Eva Monkey wrote: To me, it boils down to the characters. That's part of the reason why I prefer the series ending to the End of Eva. At the end of it all, Evangelion is not about Angels or Evas or any of that. Its about the characters. And every one of them is well defined, well portrayed, and in some way relatable to ones self.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Mon Nov 29, 2004 2:16 am Otaprince wrote: What is it about Eva? An open ending. Both TV and EoE endings are WAAAY too open. Yes, they're brilliant, artistic statements blah blah, but the simply fact is that we never got closure on Eva the way we really need.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Mon Nov 29, 2004 10:16 am Sorry if I just repeated what had already been said ^^;;, I guess I was just overeager. wow. Looking back, I think that's the longest post I've ever made! And it was about pretty much nothin' too. Neat.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Mon Nov 29, 2004 2:32 pm The action, the music, the Rei

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Mon Nov 29, 2004 3:28 pm yes, I agree. The Rei The Misato is good too though. In fact, I can't think of a single character that i dislike or find bland or uninteresting

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Mon Nov 29, 2004 3:32 pm The only chareter who is "unintresting" is the fat guy with seele

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Mon Nov 29, 2004 5:16 pm Which fat guy with SEELE? Chairman Kihl? He's super interesting! maybe the blue-ish fat guy? He's still kinda cool. I mean, he's blue...and fat, so he's got that going for him, which is nice.

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Tue Nov 30, 2004 3:05 pm BLah i guess but still hes just a fat half robot......he is kinda interesting!

For me I think its that the way the story is told you really have to think about it inorder to get it fully, and the fact that I can identify with a lot of the characters. THat or
